annotations.label: Vector space

anchor.snippet: is a set V equipped with two binary operations

annotations.note: A vector space is `Module K M` over a field; Mathlib has no separate vector-space type, using `Module` over an arbitrary semiring.

annotations.label: Vector space axioms

anchor.snippet: The axioms that addition and scalar multiplication must satisfy are the following

annotations.note: The distributivity/associativity/unit axioms are exactly the fields of the `Module` class (together with `AddCommMonoid`).

annotations.label: Vectors form an abelian group

anchor.snippet: The first four axioms mean that V is an abelian group under addition.

annotations.note: Vectors forming an abelian group under addition is the `AddCommGroup` typeclass required of a vector space.

annotations.label: Linear map

anchor.snippet: Linear maps are mappings between vector spaces that preserve the vector-space structure

annotations.note: `LinearMap` is the structure of structure-preserving maps with fields `map_add` and `map_smul`.

annotations.label: Equivalent linearity condition

anchor.snippet: An equivalent condition is that

annotations.note: Mathlib's `LinearMap` uses the equivalent two-axiom form (`map_add` and `map_smul`) rather than the single combined condition f(ax+y)=a f(x)+f(y).

annotations.label: Linear operator

anchor.snippet: is also known as a linear operator on

annotations.note: `Module.End R M` is the type of linear maps from M to itself, i.e. linear operators.

annotations.label: Isomorphism, kernel and image

anchor.snippet: A bijective linear map between two vector spaces

annotations.note: Bijective linear maps are `LinearEquiv`; kernel and image are `LinearMap.ker` and `LinearMap.range`.

annotations.label: Linear subspace

anchor.snippet: These subsets are called linear subspaces

annotations.note: Linear subspaces are `Submodule`s (defined over general rings, specializing to subspaces over fields).

annotations.label: Image and kernel are subspaces

anchor.snippet: For example, given a linear map

annotations.note: `LinearMap.range` and `LinearMap.ker` are defined directly as `Submodule`s.

annotations.label: Span of a set

anchor.snippet: form a linear subspace called the span of

annotations.note: `Submodule.span R s` is the smallest submodule containing s.

annotations.label: Linear independence

anchor.snippet: A set of vectors is linearly independent if none is in the span of the others

annotations.note: `LinearIndependent R v` formalizes linear independence of an indexed family.

annotations.label: Spanning set and basis

anchor.snippet: A set of vectors that spans a vector space is called a spanning set or generating set

annotations.note: A basis is `Basis`; spanning means `Submodule.span R s = ⊤`.

annotations.label: Basis between independent and spanning sets

anchor.snippet: More precisely, if S is a linearly independent set

annotations.note: `Basis.extend`/`Basis.extendLe` extend a linearly independent set to a basis contained in a spanning set.

annotations.label: Dimension theorem for vector spaces

anchor.snippet: this is the dimension theorem for vector spaces

annotations.note: `mk_eq_mk_of_basis` proves any two bases of a module have the same cardinality.

annotations.label: Isomorphic iff equal dimension

anchor.snippet: Moreover, two vector spaces over the same field

annotations.note: Two finite-dimensional spaces are isomorphic iff equal finrank; the general rank version `nonempty_linearEquiv_of_lift_rank_eq` also exists.

annotations.label: Finite-dimensional vector space

anchor.snippet: has a finite number of elements

annotations.note: `FiniteDimensional K V` abbreviates `Module.Finite K V` for a division ring.

annotations.label: Subspace dimension inequality

anchor.snippet: If U is a subspace of V , then dim U ≤ dim V

annotations.note: `Submodule.finrank_le` gives finrank of a submodule ≤ finrank of the ambient finite module.

annotations.label: Dimension of sum of subspaces

anchor.snippet: If U 1 and U 2 are subspaces of V , then

annotations.note: `finrank_sup_add_finrank_inf_eq` is dim(U₁+U₂)+dim(U₁∩U₂)=dim U₁+dim U₂.

annotations.label: Coordinate isomorphism from a basis

anchor.snippet: This is an isomorphism of vector spaces

annotations.note: `Basis.equivFun` is the linear equiv V ≃ₗ (ι → R) for a finite basis (`Basis.repr` is the general Finsupp version).

annotations.label: Coordinate vector / column matrix

anchor.snippet: representing a vector by its inverse image under this isomorphism

annotations.note: The coordinate vector of x is `b.repr x` (or `b.equivFun x`).

annotations.label: Matrix of a linear map

anchor.snippet: is well defined by its values on the basis elements

annotations.note: `LinearMap.toMatrix` is the equiv between linear maps and matrices given bases.

annotations.label: Matrix multiplication

anchor.snippet: Matrix multiplication is defined in such a way that the product of two matrices

annotations.note: Matrix product is the `HMul` instance, characterized by `Matrix.mul_apply`, and matches composition (`LinearMap.toMatrix'_comp`).

annotations.label: Similar matrices

anchor.snippet: Two matrices that encode the same linear transformation in different bases are called similar

annotations.note: There is no dedicated matrix-similarity definition; generic `IsConj` (conjugation by a unit in the matrix ring) captures it.

annotations.label: Similarity via row and column operations

anchor.snippet: It can be proven that two matrices are similar if and only if

annotations.note: No theorem characterizing matrix similarity via sequences of elementary row/column operations is present.

annotations.label: Similar to bordered identity

anchor.snippet: Every matrix is similar to an identity matrix possibly bordered by zero rows and zero columns

annotations.note: The rank-normal-form result (matrix equivalent to a bordered identity block) is not formalized.

annotations.label: System of linear equations

anchor.snippet: is called a system of linear equations or a linear system

annotations.note: Linear systems are written as `A *ᵥ x = b` via `Matrix.mulVec`; there is no dedicated linear-system structure.

annotations.label: Solution as preimage under T

anchor.snippet: that is an element of the preimage of

annotations.note: No named formalization of a linear system's solution set as a preimage/fiber of the associated linear map.

annotations.label: Associated homogeneous system

anchor.snippet: Let ( S′ ) be the associated homogeneous system

annotations.note: The homogeneous system A x = 0 corresponds to `Matrix.mulVecLin A` set equal to zero; no separate 'associated homogeneous system' definition.

annotations.label: Homogeneous solutions are the kernel

anchor.snippet: The solutions of ( S′ ) are exactly the elements of the kernel of

annotations.note: The solution set of A x = 0 is `LinearMap.ker A.mulVecLin` by definition (`LinearMap.mem_ker`, `Matrix.ker_mulVecLin_eq_bot_iff`).

annotations.label: Row operations preserve solutions

anchor.snippet: These row operations do not change the set of solutions of the system of equations

annotations.note: No theorem stating elementary row operations preserve a linear system's solution set.

annotations.label: Unique solution when matrix invertible

anchor.snippet: is invertible, then the system has the unique solution

annotations.note: Invertibility makes `A *ᵥ ·` injective (uniqueness) and existence is x = A⁻¹b, but there is no single packaged 'unique solution' lemma.

annotations.label: Linear endomorphism

anchor.snippet: A linear endomorphism is a linear map that maps a vector space

annotations.note: `Module.End R M` is the ring of linear endomorphisms of M.

annotations.label: Endomorphism as square matrix

anchor.snippet: such an endomorphism is represented by a square matrix of size

annotations.note: `LinearMap.toMatrixAlgEquiv` is the algebra equiv between endomorphisms and square matrices in a basis.

annotations.label: Determinant

anchor.snippet: The determinant of a square matrix A is defined to be

annotations.note: `Matrix.det` is the determinant of a square matrix.

annotations.label: Invertible iff determinant invertible

anchor.snippet: A matrix is invertible if and only if the determinant is invertible

annotations.note: `Matrix.isUnit_iff_isUnit_det` says A is a unit iff det A is a unit.

annotations.label: Cramer's rule

anchor.snippet: Cramer's rule is a closed-form expression

annotations.note: `Matrix.cramer` and `Matrix.mulVec_cramer` give Cramer's rule.

annotations.label: Determinant of an endomorphism

anchor.snippet: The determinant of an endomorphism is the determinant of the matrix representing the endomorphism

annotations.note: `LinearMap.det` is the basis-independent determinant of an endomorphism.

annotations.label: Eigenvector and eigenvalue

anchor.snippet: an eigenvector of f is a nonzero vector

annotations.note: `Module.End.HasEigenvector`/`HasEigenvalue` define eigenvectors (nonzero) and eigenvalues.

annotations.label: Characteristic polynomial

anchor.snippet: called the characteristic polynomial of the matrix

annotations.note: `Matrix.charpoly` is det(XI − M).

annotations.label: Diagonalizable

anchor.snippet: the endomorphism and the matrix are said to be diagonalizable

annotations.note: There is no 'diagonalizable' predicate; `Module.End.IsSemisimple` is the field-independent analogue and eigenvector-basis machinery exists.

annotations.label: Square-free characteristic polynomial implies diagonalizable

anchor.snippet: if the characteristic polynomial is square-free

annotations.note: Mathlib proves a squarefree annihilating polynomial implies semisimplicity (the field-independent form of diagonalizability), not diagonalizability per se.

annotations.label: Symmetric matrices are diagonalizable

anchor.snippet: A symmetric matrix is always diagonalizable

annotations.note: The spectral theorem gives unitary diagonalization of Hermitian matrices, which over ℝ are exactly the symmetric matrices.

annotations.label: Non-diagonalizable matrix

anchor.snippet: There are non-diagonalizable matrices, the simplest being

annotations.note: This is an illustrative example rather than a formal statement.

annotations.label: Frobenius normal form

anchor.snippet: The Frobenius normal form does not need to extend the field of scalars

annotations.note: The rational (Frobenius) canonical form is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Jordan normal form

anchor.snippet: The Jordan normal form requires to extension of the field of scalar

annotations.note: Jordan normal form is not formalized; only the Jordan–Chevalley (semisimple+nilpotent) decomposition exists.

annotations.label: Linear form and dual space

anchor.snippet: A linear form is a linear map from a vector space

annotations.note: `Module.Dual R M = M →ₗ[R] R` is the space of linear forms.

annotations.label: Dual basis

anchor.snippet: These linear maps form a basis of

annotations.note: `Basis.dualBasis` is the dual basis of the dual space.

annotations.label: Double dual / bidual

anchor.snippet: This defines the canonical linear map from

annotations.note: `Module.Dual.eval` is the canonical map V → V** into the bidual.

annotations.label: Canonical map to bidual is an isomorphism

anchor.snippet: This canonical map is an isomorphism if

annotations.note: `Module.evalEquiv` packages the canonical V ≃ₗ V** under reflexivity (finite-dimensionality).

annotations.label: Dual / transpose of a linear map

anchor.snippet: which is called the dual or the transpose of

annotations.note: `LinearMap.dualMap` (= `Module.Dual.transpose`) is the dual/transpose of a linear map.

annotations.label: Matrix of dual map is transpose

anchor.snippet: then the matrix of f* over the dual bases is the transpose

annotations.note: `LinearMap.toMatrix_transpose` shows the matrix of the dual map over dual bases is the transpose.

annotations.label: Inner product

anchor.snippet: Formally, an inner product is a map

annotations.note: `InnerProductSpace` (with the `Inner` class) axiomatizes the inner product.

annotations.label: Length of a vector

anchor.snippet: We can define the length of a vector

annotations.note: The inner-product norm satisfies `norm_eq_sqrt_re_inner`: ‖x‖ = √re⟪x,x⟫.

annotations.label: Cauchy–Schwarz inequality

anchor.snippet: and we can prove the Cauchy–Schwarz inequality

annotations.note: `norm_inner_le_norm` is the Cauchy–Schwarz inequality ‖⟪x,y⟫‖ ≤ ‖x‖‖y‖.

annotations.label: Orthogonality and orthonormal basis

anchor.snippet: Two vectors are orthogonal if

annotations.note: Orthonormal families are `Orthonormal` and orthonormal bases are `OrthonormalBasis`; orthogonality is ⟪x,y⟫ = 0.

annotations.label: Gram–Schmidt procedure

anchor.snippet: an orthonormal basis could be found by the Gram–Schmidt procedure

annotations.note: `gramSchmidt` and `gramSchmidtOrthonormalBasis` formalize the Gram–Schmidt process.

annotations.label: Hermitian conjugate

anchor.snippet: we can define its Hermitian conjugate

annotations.note: The Hermitian conjugate of a matrix is `Matrix.conjTranspose` (Aᴴ); the operator analogue is `ContinuousLinearMap.adjoint`.

annotations.label: Normal operator

anchor.snippet: we call T normal

annotations.note: `IsStarNormal x` states star x * x = x * star x, i.e. T commutes with its adjoint.

annotations.label: Characterization of normal matrices

anchor.snippet: It turns out that normal matrices are precisely the matrices

annotations.note: Unitary diagonalization (spectral theorem) is formalized only for Hermitian matrices; the general normal-matrix characterization is not present.

annotations.label: Module over a ring

anchor.snippet: this gives the structure called a module over

annotations.note: `Module R M` is a module over a (semi)ring R.

annotations.label: Free and finitely generated modules

anchor.snippet: The modules that have a basis are the free modules

annotations.note: `Module.Free` is having a basis and `Module.Finite` is finitely generated.

annotations.label: Submodule of free module over PID is free

anchor.snippet: over a principal ideal domain, every submodule of a free module is free

annotations.note: `Submodule.basisOfPid` produces a basis for any submodule of a finite free module over a PID.

annotations.label: Every module is a cokernel of free modules

anchor.snippet: every module is a cokernel of a homomorphism of free modules

annotations.note: The tautological presentation exhibits any module via generators and relations, i.e. as a cokernel of free modules.

annotations.label: Algebra (bilinear product)

anchor.snippet: the vector space is called an algebra

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Algebra` is the unital associative case; the general bilinear-product (possibly non-unital/non-associative) algebra is not a single typeclass.

annotations.label: Normed vector space

anchor.snippet: A normed vector space is a vector space along with a function called a norm

annotations.note: `NormedSpace` is a module with a compatible norm over a normed field.

annotations.label: Banach space

anchor.snippet: a normed vector space that is complete is known as a Banach space

annotations.note: No `BanachSpace` typeclass; a Banach space is expressed as `NormedSpace` together with `[CompleteSpace E]`.

annotations.label: Hilbert space

anchor.snippet: is known as a Hilbert space

annotations.note: No `HilbertSpace` typeclass; a Hilbert space is expressed as `InnerProductSpace` together with `[CompleteSpace E]`.
